clause_anchor.span: Routine liquidity records may be maintained to support network fees, reconciliation, and pending transfer queues. Notwithstanding Section 3, Custodian may deploy idle balances for settlement liquidity, inventory smoothing, and collateral substitutions until a withdrawal is pending.

deviations.original_text: Routine liquidity records may be maintained to support network fees, reconciliation, and pending transfer queues, but those records do not permit Custodian to borrow, deploy, or encumber Customer Assets.
deviations.mutated_text: Routine liquidity records may be maintained to support network fees, reconciliation, and pending transfer queues. Notwithstanding Section 3, Custodian may deploy idle balances for settlement liquidity, inventory smoothing, and collateral substitutions until a withdrawal is pending.

clause_anchor.span: Custodian will maintain account records showing balances credited to Customer and will make those records available through the custody portal.

deviations.original_text: Customer Assets are owned by Customer, are not property of Custodian, and are intended to be excluded from Custodian's bankruptcy estate to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law. Custodian will maintain records sufficient to identify Customer Assets as held for Customer.
deviations.mutated_text: Custodian will maintain account records showing balances credited to Customer and will make those records available through the custody portal.

clause_anchor.span: After termination, Custodian may liquidate supported balances and remit net proceeds after completion of account review, treasury reconciliation, and ordinary-course compliance screening.

deviations.original_text: Upon termination or Customer's written request, Custodian will transfer Customer Assets in kind to Customer or Customer's designated wallet or custodian within 5 business days, subject only to applicable law, a network outage, or a documented security hold notified to Customer.
deviations.mutated_text: After termination, Custodian may liquidate supported balances and remit net proceeds after completion of account review, treasury reconciliation, and ordinary-course compliance screening.

clause_anchor.span: Neither party waives any remedy by delaying enforcement, and operational notices may be delivered through the custody portal or by email to the notice contacts designated by the parties. Custodian's portal status page will be the exclusive source for operational-control information, and Custodian has no duty to provide supplemental evidence after incidents or control changes.

deviations.original_text: Neither party waives any remedy by delaying enforcement, and operational notices may be delivered through the custody portal or by email to the notice contacts designated by the parties.
deviations.mutated_text: Neither party waives any remedy by delaying enforcement, and operational notices may be delivered through the custody portal or by email to the notice contacts designated by the parties. Custodian's portal status page will be the exclusive source for operational-control information, and Custodian has no duty to provide supplemental evidence after incidents or control changes.

clause_anchor.span: "Customer Assets" means supported units recorded to Customer's primary custody account, excluding chain-split property, promotional distributions, validator proceeds, and other network-originated items unless Custodian separately credits them.

deviations.original_text: "Customer Assets" means all Digital Assets, private-key control rights, proceeds, forks, airdrops, staking rewards, and network distributions credited to or held for Customer.
deviations.mutated_text: "Customer Assets" means supported units recorded to Customer's primary custody account, excluding chain-split property, promotional distributions, validator proceeds, and other network-originated items unless Custodian separately credits them.

distractors.span: This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of New York, without regard to conflict-of-laws rules.
distractors.why_benign: The governing-law canary says New York law is acceptable for this engagement.

distractors.span: Custodian will provide an annual SOC 2 Type II report or equivalent controls report covering custody operations, key management, logical access, change management, incident response, and vendor oversight.
distractors.why_benign: The assurance canary says annual SOC 2 Type II reporting may satisfy routine assurance when follow-up rights remain available.

distractors.span: Routine liquidity records may be maintained to support network fees, reconciliation, and pending transfer queues, but those records do not permit Custodian to borrow, deploy, or encumber Customer Assets.
distractors.why_benign: Administrative liquidity records are permitted because they do not authorize use of the customer's assets.

missing_info.context_anchor: Customer also needs an internal policy describing wallet whitelisting approval before funds move.
